title: Cheesy Chicken Broccoli Bake
description: A creamy, cheesy casserole that turns leftover chicken and a head of broccoli into a whole supper. Comfort food that feeds a crowd.

ingredients.note: 1 1/2 cups for the sauce, 1/2 cup for topping

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F and grease a 9x13 baking dish.
Melt the butter in a saucepan. Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Whisk in the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Slowly whisk in the chicken broth and milk. Simmer 3 to 5 minutes, until slightly thickened.
Stir in 1 1/2 cups of the cheddar and all of the Parmesan until melted.
Add the onion powder, garlic powder, paprika, dried mustard, salt, and pepper.
In a large bowl, combine the chicken, rice, broccoli, and cheese sauce.
Spread the mixture into the prepared baking dish.
Top with the remaining 1/2 cup cheddar cheese.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until hot and bubbly.
For a golden top, broil for 2 to 3 minutes (optional).
Let rest 5 to 10 minutes before serving.
notes: Great for leftover or rotisserie chicken.
Frozen broccoli works — just thaw and drain it well.
Can be assembled ahead of time and baked later.
leftovers: Good leftovers — keeps 3 to 4 days in the refrigerator and reheats beautifully.
storage: Cover and refrigerate up to 4 days, or freeze up to 3 months. Reheat covered at 350°F until hot in the middle.
whyWeChoseThis: Turns leftover or rotisserie chicken into a full supper.
One dish feeds six to eight — good for company or the freezer.
A whole serving of vegetables tucked into the cheese sauce.

editorialNotes.note: Added to the cookbook — a cheesy way to use up leftover chicken and a head of broccoli.
